title: AnalyticalQuery object anchor

summary: Analytics query/reporting anchor for FO work: source object/query, KPI definition, filter, drilldown, authorization, extraction mode and data freshness must be specified before reports are accepted.

summary: Payment proposal object anchor for AP FOs that need payment eligibility, block reason, due date, payment method and exception handling context.
title: AP payment proposal object anchor

summary: Billing document object anchor for FO work: billing document identity, payer, company code, billing type, amounts, currency, accounting status and output dispatch context should be separated.
title: Billing document object anchor

summary: Object anchor for business partner master-data FO sections.
title: BusinessPartner master-data object

summary: Business Partner object anchor for FO sections where one legal or natural party may carry customer, supplier or other roles. Keep role/company-code and duplicate-check assumptions visible.
title: Business Partner master-data object

keywords: Business role and catalog object anchor
queries: Business role and catalog object anchor

summary: Object anchor for business role, catalog and restriction field FO sections.
title: Business role and catalog object anchor

keywords: Business role and catalog assignment object anchor
queries: Business role and catalog assignment object anchor

summary: Object anchor for authorization FOs: business role, business catalog, restriction value, app access, user assignment and test-user evidence should be explicit and tenant-verified.

summary: Object anchor for communication scenario, arrangement and credential FO sections.
title: CommunicationArrangement object anchor

summary: Communication arrangement object anchor for FO work: scenario, communication system, user/auth method, inbound/outbound direction and test evidence must be captured without storing secrets or tenant endpoints.
title: Communication arrangement object anchor

summary: Generic company-code customizing anchor absorbed from SAP Field Atlas, centered on T001 and the BUKRS company-code identifier.
title: Company Code customizing anchor

summary: Object anchor for custom field definition, usage and lifecycle FO sections.
title: CustomField extension object anchor

summary: Custom field extension object anchor for FO work: business context, field type, semantic meaning, UI placement, API/report exposure, publish status and fallback behavior must be explicit and tenant-verified.
title: Custom field extension object anchor

summary: Customer role object anchor for FOs that need BP-to-customer extension, organization or company-code assumptions. Do not assume role availability without tenant/release verification.
title: Customer master-data role object

summary: Equipment is a maintainable technical object in EAM/Plant Maintenance context. Treat equipment identifiers, category, planning plant, planner group, location/account assignment and status as tenant-verified fields, not universal values. Confidence: medium internal_derived starter anchor. For status questions, keep the equipment anchor selected together with system status, user status and status profile caveats. Supports IH08 equipment list/search questions so the equipment object anchor remains selected with the transaction context.
title: EAM equipment technical object

summary: Equipment BOM starter anchor: equipment-specific BOM context links a technical object to material components/spares for planning and maintenance. Exact BOM usage/category, alternatives and validity are tenant-specific.
title: Equipment BOM spare-parts context

summary: Functional location is the structured location/hierarchy anchor often related to equipment. Use it as context for equipment placement and technical-object hierarchy, with tenant verification for structure indicators and labels. Confidence: medium internal_derived.
title: EAM functional location technical object

summary: Functional-location BOM starter anchor: location-level BOM context links a functional location to reusable component/spare structures. Exact hierarchy, BOM usage/category and validity require tenant verification.
title: Functional-location BOM spare-parts context

summary: Maintenance order is the execution anchor for planned or corrective maintenance work. Treat order type, operation structure, components, confirmations, technical completion and settlement behavior as tenant-specific.
title: EAM maintenance order execution object

summary: Maintenance order component anchor: planned materials/components on a maintenance order can drive reservation, availability and goods issue questions. Do not invent movement types, storage locations or reservation behavior; verify in tenant.
title: Maintenance order component material context

summary: Maintenance plan is the planning anchor for recurring/preventive maintenance. Treat strategy, cycle, call horizon, scheduling parameters and generated call/order behavior as tenant-specific and source-verified before final FO use.
title: EAM maintenance plan

summary: Measuring points and counters capture condition/counter readings used in inspection and condition-based maintenance scenarios. Exact reading categories, units, limits and follow-on behavior require tenant verification.
title: EAM measuring point and counter

summary: Permit/safety handling is a process and compliance caveat around maintenance execution. Do not assume permit types, approvals or lockout/safety steps without tenant and regulatory context.
title: EAM permit and safety caveat

summary: Settlement is a costing/controlling caveat for maintenance orders, not a safe generic assumption. Settlement receivers, profiles and period-end behavior require tenant-specific FI/CO evidence.
title: EAM settlement caveat

summary: EAM spare-part material anchor: a material/product can be used as a maintenance spare or component for equipment, functional-location or order planning. Confidence: medium internal_derived; verify plant, stock, valuation, serial/batch and tenant configuration before implementation.
title: EAM spare-part material object

summary: Status profile is the tenant/configuration anchor that can control allowed user statuses and business transactions. Public answers must not invent profile names or allowed status values. Confidence: medium internal_derived. Supports Dutch IE03 questions about where user statuses and system statuses are visible by keeping status profile caveat selected.
title: EAM status profile

summary: System status is SAP/system-controlled status context on a technical object. Do not describe it as freely configurable user status. Exact displayed values depend on object lifecycle and tenant/release. Confidence: medium internal_derived.
title: EAM system status

summary: Task list context describes reusable maintenance operations, responsible work centers, component/spare expectations and tools/resources that can be assigned or referenced in planning/execution.
title: EAM maintenance task list

summary: Technical completion is an order lifecycle milestone/caveat for stopping or closing technical execution context. Do not equate it with financial settlement or business completion without tenant process evidence.
title: EAM technical completion lifecycle state

summary: Technical-object status context groups system status and user status information for equipment/functional locations. In display questions, select this object plus the system/user status rules and caveats. Confidence: medium internal_derived.
title: Technical object status container

summary: User status is customer/tenant-configurable status context controlled through status profile/customizing. Do not present a user-status list as universal SAP standard. Confidence: medium internal_derived.
title: EAM user status

summary: Work center is a planning/execution responsibility and capacity anchor for task list and maintenance order operations. Exact cost center/capacity/formula/scheduling behavior is tenant-specific.
title: EAM work center

keywords: SAP GUI command field navigation prefix context
queries: SAP GUI command field navigation prefix context

summary: SAP GUI command-field prefixes such as /n and /o modify navigation behavior: /n starts a transaction in the current session, /o opens/starts a transaction in another session/window where available. They are prefixes, not part of the transaction code. Confidence: medium internal_derived; verify in target GUI/client behavior. Supports Dutch consultant questions such as welke transacties om equipment te openen/zoeken in SAP GUI, including NIE01 versus /nIE01 correction.
title: SAP GUI command field navigation prefix context

keywords: SAP ECC DD02T full export profile from anonymized metadata source
queries: SAP ECC DD02T full export profile from anonymized metadata source

summary: An anonymized profile of a full SAP ECC DD02T table-text export. The profile captures coverage, language distribution and safe curation rules without publishing the full system table/view name inventory.
title: SAP ECC DD02T full export profile from anonymized metadata source

keywords: SAP ECC DDIC table catalog from anonymized DD02T and DD02L extracts
queries: SAP ECC DDIC table catalog from anonymized DD02T and DD02L extracts

summary: An anonymized SAP ECC DDIC table catalog variant for table/view descriptions and technical table metadata, filtered to the DD03VT field-catalog scope curated in this repository.
title: SAP ECC DDIC table catalog from anonymized DD02T and DD02L extracts

summary: Trace object for app-answer context: app name/source, process or business object, business catalog/role, scope item, authorization caveat and local tenant evidence status.
title: Fiori app traceability object

keywords: SAP GUI ALV grid spreadsheet export behavior
queries: SAP GUI ALV grid spreadsheet export behavior

summary: ALV grid spreadsheet export can remember a user-specific chosen format when the user selects an always-use option. In an ALV output screen, the OK-code/function code &RESET_EXCEL can reset that remembered spreadsheet export format so the selection dialog appears again. Confidence: high for SAP Help-described ALV reset pattern; verify whether a specific report uses standard ALV and whether the current SAP GUI/client honors the function code.
title: SAP GUI ALV grid spreadsheet export behavior

keywords: SAP GUI command field and OK-code field
queries: SAP GUI command field and OK-code field

summary: The SAP GUI command field, also called the OK-code field in many consultant conversations, accepts transaction navigation commands such as /nSE16N, session commands such as /oSE16N and screen/report-specific function codes such as &RESET_EXCEL. Confidence: medium internal_derived with public SAP Help pointers for ALV spreadsheet reset behavior; verify exact client behavior in the target SAP GUI/system.
title: SAP GUI command field and OK-code field

summary: API endpoint object anchor for FO work: API name, entity/resource, operation, direction, key fields, payload ownership, error handling and release evidence should be explicit.
title: Integration API endpoint object anchor

summary: Batch-management anchor for materials requiring batch traceability. Exact batch level/activation and process use are tenant-specific.
title: Batch-managed material caveat

summary: Generic material/product master-data anchor absorbed from SAP Field Atlas, centered on MARA and the material number/product number identifier.
title: Material Master / Product master data

summary: MRP view anchor for planning-related material questions such as MRP type/controller and plant planning caveats. Do not assume exact planning configuration.
title: Material MRP view

summary: Plant-specific material data anchor for MRP, purchasing, valuation/accounting and logistics checks. Use as starter context, not exact configuration proof.
title: Plant-specific material data

summary: Material/product master anchor for questions about product/material identity, base unit, product type/material type and organizational extensions. Exact field names and mandatory views require tenant/release verification. Supports broad material master purchasing sales plant starter questions with caveats.
title: Material/product master object

summary: Purchasing view anchor for procurement-relevant material data such as purchasing group or procurement-relevant material defaults. Verify fields/release before design.
title: Material purchasing view

summary: Sales/distribution material view anchor for selling/delivery-related material questions. Tenant sales org/channel and field behavior require verification.
title: Material sales/distribution view

summary: Serial-number anchor for material/equipment traceability questions. Exact serial profile/equipment integration must be verified in target tenant/system.
title: Serial-number managed material caveat

summary: Storage-location material context for inventory/storage-specific checks. Exact storage location, bin and MRP-area behavior must be verified in tenant.
title: Storage-location material data

summary: Material type/product type influences which fields/views and process behavior are relevant. Do not invent tenant-specific material types or mandatory field settings.
title: Material type / product type caveat

summary: Valuation/accounting anchor for material value, valuation class and price-control questions. Do not invent valuation class/account determination; require tenant verification.
title: Material valuation/accounting view

summary: Object anchor for migration project, migration object and load-file FO sections.
title: MigrationProject object anchor

summary: Object anchor for migration project run phases: prepare/extract, map, validate, simulate, load, correct errors and reconcile.
title: Migration project run object anchor

summary: Object anchor for a downloaded migration template: template label, source column, target key, transformation rule, mandatory flag and verification note.
title: Migration template object anchor

summary: Object anchor for value-source and customizing-controlled migration fields, such as company code. Values should be checked against the target tenant before migration use.
title: Migration value-source mapping object anchor

summary: Object anchor for output request, receiver and channel FO sections.
title: OutputRequest object anchor

summary: Product/material object anchor for FOs involving product identity, product type/category, unit-of-measure, status and migration labels.
title: Product / material master-data object

summary: Purchase order object anchor for FO work: supplier, purchasing organization, company code, item quantity/value, delivery date, confirmation, goods receipt and invoice receipt context should be separated.
title: Purchase order object anchor

summary: Object anchor for purchase requisition FO field mapping and approval tests.
title: PurchaseRequisition business object

summary: Purchase requisition object anchor for FO work: requisition/item identity, material/service description, quantity, plant, requester, account assignment, workflow state and conversion readiness should be explicit.
title: Purchase requisition object anchor

summary: Object anchor for sales order processing, output and fulfillment FO sections.
title: SalesOrder business object

summary: Sales order object anchor for FO work: order/item identity, sales area, sold-to/ship-to, material, quantity, net value, delivery hold, billing hold and output relevance should be explicit.
title: Sales order object anchor

summary: Output item context for sales/billing FOs: business document, output type, channel, recipient, form template, dispatch timing, processing status and error handling must be explicit and tenant-verified.
title: Sales output item object anchor

summary: Lifecycle anchor for SAP implementation phases. It helps an agent decide whether the right evidence is a process/design decision, a configuration decision, system proof or run/support evidence.
title: SAP Activate lifecycle context

summary: Configuration/customizing anchor for distinguishing classic SPRO/IMG, Public Cloud-oriented configuration/CBC/SSCUI-like surfaces, master data, extensions and code. Concrete configuration values remain target-tenant evidence.
title: SAP configuration surface context

summary: Foundation anchor for explaining SAP as a broad ERP/business process platform whose answers depend on product generation, edition, release, lifecycle phase, system landscape, configuration, organization and evidence.
title: SAP context foundation

summary: Anchor for configuration/customizing context. SPRO/IMG, CBC and SSCUI-like configuration surfaces can control behavior, allowed values and process variants; concrete values remain target-system evidence, not generic SAP truth.
title: SAP customizing and configuration context

summary: Anchor for separating implementation lifecycle phase from system landscape. Before a system exists, questions belong to Discover/Prepare/Explore-style design evidence; after systems exist, DEV/QAS/PRD/client evidence matters.
title: SAP landscape and lifecycle context

keywords: SAP organization, process and surface context
queries: SAP organization, process and surface context

summary: Anchor for connecting organizational units, process/capability maps and interaction surfaces. Company codes, plants, sales orgs, Fiori apps, SAP GUI transactions, APIs/events and migration templates are different lenses over the same implementation context.
title: SAP organization, process and surface context

summary: Organization scope anchor. Company code, controlling area, plant, storage location, sales organization, distribution channel, division and purchasing organization can scope process behavior, master data and validation.
title: SAP organization structure scope context

summary: Landscape anchor for separating DEV/build, QAS/test/acceptance and PRD/run evidence. System/client names and actual landscapes are customer-specific; generic answers can only describe the role of each layer.
title: SAP system landscape context

summary: Curated object anchor for supplier invoice workflow FOs. Use this item to keep field mappings, decision rules and test data tied to the same business object.
title: SupplierInvoice business object

summary: Supplier invoice object anchor for AP FOs: invoice identity, supplier, company code, fiscal period, amounts, tax, approval status, block status and payment readiness must be separated.
title: Supplier invoice AP object anchor

summary: Supplier role object anchor for FOs that need BP-to-supplier extension, procurement organization, company-code or payment-term assumptions.
title: Supplier master-data role object

summary: Object anchor for classifying whether a field value is a direct identifier, customizing-controlled value, code-list value, derived/defaulted value or tenant-specific lookup.
title: Value-source classification object
